title: 4 Interac e-TransferÂ® Limits and Central 1 Limit Groups
body_text:
4	Interac e-Transfer
Â®
Limits and Central 1 Limit Groups
4.1	Interac e-Transfer Limits
There is a maximum dollar value of Interac e-Transfer transactions that an Interac e-Transfer profile can send
Per transaction
Over a 24-hour period
Over a 7-day period
Over a 30-day period
Outgoing limits are shared between Send Money and Request Money transactions. For example,  your customerâs outgoing limit determines whether they can fulfill a request.
Interac manages limits for each Interac e-Transfer profile. Interac stores information on the total value of Interac e-Transfer transactions that a customer has sent over a 24-hour, 7-day, and 30-day period. If the total value of your customerâs outgoing Interac e-Transfer transactions exceeds any of the four limits (per transaction, per 24-hour, per 7-day, or per 30-day),
Interac denies the customerâs request to send funds.
The 24-hour, 7-day, and 30-day limits are outgoing rolling limits based on rolling time periods. See Section 2.1, Definitions, for the definitions and examples of Rolling Limits and Rolling Time Periods.
The dollar value for each limit is determined by the financial institutionâs Limit Group. Financial institutions can change their current Limit Group. See Section 4.2, Limit Groups, for more information.
4.2	Limit Groups
Interac
e-Transfer limit groups enable your financial institution to offer different limits than the default ones currently set by Central 1 at Interac
.
Participation in limit groups is optional. Depending on the your customersâ needs, you can use the default limits currently set by Central 1 at Interac
,
or you can select one of Central 1âs custom limit groups. See Interac e-Transfer
Â®
Limit Groups, Form 9369.
